jokes.text: AUNTIE RAE GAVE ME-- SHE GAVE ME THE FINGER AS SHE WAS DRIVING AWAY. YEAH. DID SHE REALLY? OH GOD. 'FUCK YOU, LARRY.'
jokes.setup: Larry explaining the breakdown of his relationship with Loretta
jokes.punchline: Even Loretta's elderly aunt is giving Larry the finger and cursing at him
jokes.explanation: Establishes Larry's universal unlikability - even elderly relatives hate him enough to curse. Character-specific and escalates the absurdity.

jokes.text: THOSE REUNION SHOWS, THEY'RE SO LAME, REALLY. THEY NEVER WORK. THE ACTORS ARE 10 YEARS OLDER. IT JUST DOESN'T LOOK RIGHT.
jokes.setup: Larry dismissing reunion shows as pathetic
jokes.punchline: Larry is literally about to do exactly what he's criticizing
jokes.explanation: Perfect meta-comedy - Larry criticizing the exact concept of the episode. High irony and character hypocrisy, well-structured.

jokes.text: YOU ARE A PICTURE OF HAPPINESS. SHE DOES HAVE A VERY GOOD DISPOSITION. SHE'S BUBBLY. WOULDN'T YOU SAY THAT? BUBBLY. YEAH, I THINK SO.
jokes.setup: Susie complaining she's tired, achy, and miserable
jokes.punchline: Larry and Jeff sarcastically calling this miserable person 'bubbly' and 'a picture of happiness'
jokes.explanation: Classic deadpan irony - saying the opposite of what's obviously true. Good execution but not groundbreaking.

jokes.text: WHAT ARE YOU, A FUCKING DOCTOR? HOW DO YOU KNOW THESE THINGS? I'M A HYPOCHONDRIAC, SO I'M AWARE OF THIS STUFF.
jokes.setup: Susie incredulous that Larry would know about Lyme disease
jokes.punchline: Larry's matter-of-fact admission that his neuroses make him medically knowledgeable
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Larry's hypochondria becomes useful. Well-structured reveal with economy.

jokes.text: I CAN'T BELIEVE IT. THIS IS THE SECOND TIME I'VE RUN INTO YOU IN, WHAT, THREE WEEKS?
jokes.setup: Ex-spouses running into each other
jokes.punchline: Larry's awkward over-emphasis on how unusual it is to see his ex-wife
jokes.explanation: Realistic awkward divorced couple interaction. Functional but not particularly inventive.

jokes.text: MY GOD. YOU HAVE TO WRITE A PART FOR ME. OH HO, YOU'VE GOT IT. OKAY. I'M NOT KIDDING. YEAH. I'M NOT KIDDING. YOU'RE NOT KIDDING. OKAY, FINE. NO, I'M NOT.
jokes.setup: Cheryl asking for a part in the reunion show
jokes.punchline: Her desperate over-insistence that she's not kidding, revealing how badly she wants it
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy - Cheryl's desperation becomes uncomfortably obvious through repetition.

jokes.text: OH, I'M JUST-- I'M DOING PILATES, I'M LEARNING JAPANESE AND I'M INTO THE LAKERS. I AM FINALLY APPRECIATING-- NOW YOU'RE INTO BASKETBALL? AFTER I LEAVE?
jokes.setup: Cheryl listing her new post-divorce interests
jokes.punchline: Larry's bitter realization she's now into all the things he probably wanted her to like
jokes.explanation: Relatable divorced-person comedy - the ex develops interests you always wanted them to have. Character-specific and earned.

jokes.text: I'M SURE YOU WILL. WHY WOULDN'T YOU? WHY WOULDN'T I? YEAH.
jokes.setup: Larry saying he might run into her at the Lakers game
jokes.punchline: Cheryl's deadpan acknowledgment of Larry's tendency to show up everywhere
jokes.explanation: Subtle character moment - Cheryl knows Larry well enough to predict his stalkerish behavior. Economy in dialogue.

jokes.text: REALLY? 'CAUSE I CAN TRY IT A DIFFERENT WAY IF YOU WANT ME TO. I CAN-- NO, DON'T CHANGE A THING. IT'S PERFECT.
jokes.setup: Larry watching what appears to be a Seinfeld reunion show rehearsal
jokes.punchline: The overly eager, insecure actor response to direction
jokes.explanation: Meta moment showing the reunion show in production. Realistic actor behavior but not particularly inventive.

jokes.text: [Dream sequence with cast praising Larry]
jokes.setup: Larry fantasizing about doing the reunion show
jokes.punchline: Everyone in the dream praising Larry excessively for his brilliant ideas
jokes.explanation: Brilliant fantasy sequence showing Larry's ego and delusions. The over-the-top praise feels both absurd and believable for a fantasy.

jokes.text: WELL, IT'S MAKING ME A BIT DIZZY, BUT I'LL DO ANYTHING FOR YOU, LARRY.
jokes.setup: Larry praising Michael's entrance slide
jokes.punchline: Michael admitting the move makes him dizzy but he'll endure it for Larry
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Michael - dedication to physical comedy despite personal cost. Economical delivery.

jokes.text: YEAH, AND THAT NEW LINE-- THE KUMQUAT AND THE QUAIL? YOU'RE AMAZING. YEAH, AMAZING. AMAZING. YEAH, YOU'RE REALLY UNBELIEVABLE, LARRY.
jokes.setup: The cast praising Larry's writing
jokes.punchline: The increasingly over-the-top, synchronized praise becoming uncomfortable
jokes.explanation: Good escalation comedy - the praise becomes uncomfortably excessive. Well-structured build.

jokes.text: IN THE 11 YEARS SINCE THE SHOW'S BEEN OFF THE AIR, HE'S GOTTEN MARRIED, DIVORCED AND NOW HE'S GONNA TRY TO GET HIS WIFE BACK.
jokes.setup: Larry pitching George's storyline
jokes.punchline: The storyline is transparently about Larry's own situation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-comedy - Larry is literally describing his own plan while pretending it's fiction. Perfect character integration and economy.

jokes.text: I KNOW WHAT YOU'RE UP TO. YOU'RE SO FULL OF IT. WE'RE SITTING UP THERE, YOU SAY, 'I'LL DO IT,' AND I'M GOING 'REALLY? YOU'LL DO IT?' AND LITERALLY, IT HIT ME THE SECOND WE WALKED OUT OF THAT MEETING.
jokes.setup: Jeff realizing Larry's true motivation
jokes.punchline: Jeff calling out Larry's transparent scheme to win back Cheryl
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Jeff as the voice of reason calling out Larry's obvious manipulation. Well-structured revelation.

jokes.text: PRETTY FUCKING BRILLIANT IF YOU ASK ME.
jokes.setup: Jeff exposing Larry's scheme
jokes.punchline: Jeff's immediate admiration for the manipulation
jokes.explanation: Perfect character reversal - Jeff goes from calling Larry out to admiring the scheme. Excellent economy.

jokes.text: WHENEVER A SITCOM DOES A REUNION EPISODE YOU SAY, 'ISN'T IT PATHETIC?' DESPERATE, PATHETIC. DESPERATE? WHEN WE WOULD WATCH SHOWS-- OTHER SHOWS-- WE'D SEE THEM DO REUNION SHOWS, YOU WOULD LOOK AND YOU'D MAKE THAT FACE-- YOU KNOW, THAT VERY JUDGMENTAL FACE OF YOURS...
jokes.setup: Jerry confronting Larry about his hypocrisy
jokes.punchline: Jerry perfectly describing Larry's judgmental behavior about the exact thing he's now proposing
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback and character work - Jerry knows Larry well enough to call out his exact hypocritical behavior patterns.

jokes.text: I KNOW, BUT IT'S LIKE YOU'RE GOING BACK TO AN OLD GIRLFRIEND AND SAYING 'HEY, MAYBE I CAN MAKE IT WORK NOW, 10 YEARS LATER.' DOES THAT EVER WORK IN ANY RELATIONSHIP THAT YOU CAN THINK OF?
jokes.setup: Jerry making an analogy about reunion shows
jokes.punchline: The analogy perfectly describes Larry's actual plan with Cheryl
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-comedy - Jerry unknowingly describes Larry's exact situation. Perfect ironic structure and universal truth.

jokes.text: ROBERT WAGNER AND NATALIE WOOD. HOW ABOUT THAT? THEY GOT DIVORCED AND THEY GOT BACK TOGETHER. AND THEN SHE SLIPPED OFF THE BOAT AND DIED. SO HOW DID THAT WORK OUT?
jokes.setup: Larry citing a successful reconciliation example
jokes.punchline: Jerry pointing out it ended with mysterious death
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy with perfect misdirection. Larry's example backfires spectacularly. Great structure and timing.

jokes.text: AND CHRISTOPHER WALKEN? HOW DO YOU EXPLAIN THAT? I DON'T KNOW WHAT HE WAS DOING ON THE BOAT.
jokes.setup: Jerry mentioning Christopher Walken in relation to Natalie Wood's death
jokes.punchline: Larry's confused, matter-of-fact response about the mysterious circumstances
jokes.explanation: Great follow-up to the dark comedy - Larry's confused literalism about a famous mystery. Perfect economy.

jokes.text: YEAH. I CAN'T ARGUE WITH THAT.
jokes.setup: Larry saying they'll do the reunion in a way that won't be lame
jokes.punchline: Jerry's deadpan acceptance of the completely non-specific assurance
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan moment - Jerry's logical surrender to Larry's non-logic. Perfect economy but not groundbreaking.

jokes.text: Because we'll do it in a way that won't be lame. We will.
jokes.setup: Jerry asking why their reunion won't be lame
jokes.punchline: Larry's completely circular and unconvincing logic
jokes.explanation: Larry's weak attempt at logic when he has no real argument. The emptiness of the statement is the joke.

jokes.text: YOU KNOW WHO'D BE GREAT FOR THAT IS MEG RYAN. ARE YOU KIDDING ME? YES, MEG RYAN. MEG RYAN? I SAW HER IN THIS THING THE OTHER NIGHT. SHE GOT UP AND TALKED, SHE LOOKS GREAT. SHE'S FUNNY. SHE'S GREAT.
jokes.setup: Casting discussion for George's wife
jokes.punchline: Jerry's enthusiastic, over-detailed pitch for Meg Ryan while Larry is horrified
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy - Jerry's oblivious enthusiasm vs. Larry's horror at a major star ruining his plan.

jokes.text: HE IS A-- YOU KNOW, HE'S A JERKY, SCHMUCKY LITTLE CHARACTER. ALL RIGHT? HE'S JUST A BUFFOON.
jokes.setup: Discussing George's character
jokes.punchline: Jason brutally describing his own character with perfect accuracy
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-moment - Jason honestly assessing his own character. Self-aware and character-specific.

jokes.text: BECAUSE HE'S SELFISH, HE'S STUPID, HE LIES... HE'S NOT STUPID. ...HE'S ABRASIVE, HE'S INEPT. HE'S FUNNY. EVERYTHING YOU JUST SAID IS FUNNY.
jokes.setup: Jason listing George's terrible qualities
jokes.punchline: Larry defending the character by saying all the terrible qualities are what make him funny
jokes.explanation: Good meta-comedy about comedy itself - all the worst character traits are what make George funny. Solid structure.

jokes.text: IT COULD MAKE UP FOR THE FINALE. THAT'S FOR SURE. WHAT DOES THAT MEAN? 'MAKE UP FOR THE FIN'-- THERE'S NOTHING TO MAKE UP FOR.
jokes.setup: Jason suggesting the reunion could redeem the controversial finale
jokes.punchline: Larry's defensive denial that the finale needs redemption
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-commentary on the real Seinfeld finale controversy. Larry's defensiveness is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: YOU DON'T NEED TO CONCERN YOURSELF SO MUCH WITH WHO'S PLAYING GEORGE'S WIFE. IT'S NOT CONCERN. I'M EXCITED. NEXT THING I KNOW, YOU'RE GONNA BE-- WHAT, YOU'RE GONNA BE WRITING WITH ME.
jokes.setup: Jason getting excited about casting ideas
jokes.punchline: Larry's paranoid escalation that Jason will want to write
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Larry's controlling nature and paranoia about collaborative input.

jokes.text: WHAT, ARE WE FAVORED NATIONS ALL OF A SUDDEN FOR LUNCH? NO, BUT WHAT IS THE BIG DEAL IF WE BOTH LEAVE THE SAME AMOUNT?
jokes.setup: Larry wanting to coordinate tips
jokes.punchline: Jason's sarcastic 'favored nations' reference (Hollywood contract term)
jokes.explanation: Good industry-specific humor - 'favored nations' is a Hollywood contract term. Shows character knowledge and good economy.

jokes.text: [Larry's elaborate tip secrecy ritual]
jokes.setup: The tip coordination argument with Jason
jokes.punchline: Larry's theatrical performance of hiding his tip amount
jokes.explanation: Brilliant physical comedy showcasing Larry's pettiness and paranoia. The elaborate ritual for such a mundane action is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: NOW I'M FOLDING THAT NUMBER AND I'M PUTTING IT INSIDE MY LITTLE LEATHER CHECK HOLDER.
jokes.setup: Jason writing his tip amount
jokes.punchline: Jason's exaggerated, theatrical process of hiding his tip amount from Larry
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - Jason turns tip secrecy into an elaborate performance. Visual comedy through description.

jokes.text: I HAVE TO TELL YOU, LARRY, I'VE NEVER SEEN A REUNION SHOW THAT WAS NOT TACKY. YEAH, I-- I MEAN, EVERY TIME YOU SEE ONE OF THESE REUNION SHOWS, IT FEELS LIKE THEY'RE DOING IT FOR THE WRONG REASONS.
jokes.setup: Julia expressing skepticism about reunion shows
jokes.punchline: She's unknowingly describing exactly what Larry is doing - wrong reasons
jokes.explanation: Good meta-irony - Julia describing Larry's exact situation without knowing it. Character-driven and well-structured.

jokes.text: I've never seen a reunion show that was not tacky.
jokes.setup: Larry pitching reunion show ideas to Julia
jokes.punchline: Julia echoing Larry's original position about reunion shows
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing how Larry's previous opinions are coming back to haunt him from multiple people.

jokes.text: IS IT CHOMPING OR CHAMPING? I THINK IT'S CHOMP-- CHAMPING? WHAT IS CHAMPING? I THINK IT'S SPELLED 'CHAMPING' BUT PRONOUNCED 'CHOMPING.' NO, IT'S SPELLED 'CHOMPING' AND IT'S PRONOUNCED 'CHOMPING.' NOT SO SURE. I'M SO SURE.
jokes.setup: Confusion over 'champing/chomping at the bit'
jokes.punchline: Escalating certainty about something completely trivial
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry-style argument about meaningless details. Character-appropriate but not groundbreaking.

jokes.text: NO, I DIDN'T GO TO A BIRTHDAY PARTY. REALLY? YEAH, REALLY. THAT'S NOT WHAT YOUR MOTHER SAID. I DIDN'T GO TO A BIRTHDAY PARTY. HUH. INTERESTING.
jokes.setup: Larry interrogating Julia's daughter about the birthday party
jokes.punchline: Larry's ominous 'interesting' when catching Julia in what seems like a lie
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy - Larry interrogating a child to catch her mother lying. Perfectly inappropriate behavior.

jokes.text: HUH? DO YOU LIKE THAT IDEA? HUH? YEAH. GOOD IDEA, RIGHT? OH, YEAH. I MEAN, AM I CRAZY? TELL ME. WHAT DO YOU THINK? HMM? YEAH. AM I CRAZY? HMM? AM I CRAZY? DO YOU THINK I'M CRAZY? I DON'T KNOW.
jokes.setup: Larry pitching ideas to an increasingly non-responsive Michael
jokes.punchline: Michael's complete disengagement while Larry desperately seeks validation
jokes.explanation: Excellent cringe comedy - the escalating desperation vs. complete non-engagement. Well-structured awkwardness.

jokes.text: OKAY, I'M GLAD YOU SAID THAT. I'M GLAD YOU THINK I'M CRAZY. THAT MEANS IT'S A GOOD IDEA. IF YOU THINK IT'S A CRAZY IDEA, THEN IT'S A GOOD IDEA.
jokes.setup: Michael saying he doesn't know if Larry is crazy
jokes.punchline: Larry's convoluted logic that crazy equals good in comedy
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Larry's desperate rationalization and comedy philosophy. Absurd but character-specific logic.

jokes.text: WOULD YOU MIND TELLING ME HOW MUCH TIP THE GUY I WAS WITH LEFT YOU? OH. WE DON'T DO THAT. YOU DON'T DO WHAT? WE DON'T SHARE THAT INFORMATION WITH CUSTOMERS.
jokes.setup: Larry asking the waiter about Jason's tip
jokes.punchline: The waiter treating tip amounts like classified information
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the tip obsession. The waiter's professional boundaries vs. Larry's neurotic curiosity.

jokes.text: JUST SCRATCH YOUR FACE WITH YOUR FINGER IF IT WAS OVER $12. GO AHEAD, JUST SCRATCH IT. WAS IT OVER $12?
jokes.setup: Larry trying to get information from the waiter
jokes.punchline: Larry creating an elaborate secret signal system for tip amounts
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation - Larry's obsession becomes completely absurd. Perfect character-driven comedy with great visual element.

jokes.text: LET ME ASK YOU WAS IT OVER $15? JUST TUG ON YOUR TIE UP HERE. WAS IT OVER $15?
jokes.setup: Continuing the secret signal system
jokes.punchline: Larry escalating both the amount and the absurdity of the signals
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of the signal gag. Each iteration becomes more absurd while revealing Larry's growing obsession.

jokes.text: YEAH, IF IT'S OVER $30, I WANT YOU TO RAISE THAT CUP OVER THAT POT.
jokes.setup: The signal system reaching peak absurdity
jokes.punchline: Larry now assigning complex physical gestures to specific dollar amounts
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation climax - the signals become increasingly elaborate. Great visual comedy and character obsession.

jokes.text: WHO SAVED HER LIFE? WHO SAVED HER LIFE? CAN YOU-- I SAVED HER LIFE. YOU SAVED HER LIFE. DR. DAVID. DR. DAVID. PAGING DR. DAVID.
jokes.setup: Larry wanting credit for diagnosing Susie's Lyme disease
jokes.punchline: Jeff's over-the-top mock-medical announcements celebrating Larry
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Larry's hypochondriac knowledge paying off. Jeff's mock celebration is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: AND YOU... ARE GONNA PLAY GEORGE'S EX-WIFE. I AM? DO YOU WANT TO? OH MY GOD! HUH? ARE YOU KIDDING ME?
jokes.setup: Larry offering Cheryl the role
jokes.punchline: Cheryl's explosive excitement and gratitude
jokes.explanation: Good payoff moment - Larry's manipulation working perfectly. Cheryl's genuine excitement contrasts with Larry's ulterior motives.

jokes.text: LOOK AT THIS. YOU'VE GOTTA BE KIDDING. CAN YOU BELIEVE THESE SEATS THIS GUY GAVE US?
jokes.setup: Sandy promised great Lakers seats
jokes.punchline: The seats are terrible - high up in the nosebleeds
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Larry's confrontation. Visual comedy of terrible seats vs. promised courtside treatment.

jokes.text: OH, HE DOES HAVE COURTSIDE SEATS. HE'S SITTING IN THEM-- HE AND DAVID SPADE. WHAT? HE'S SITTING NEXT TO DAVID SPADE.
jokes.setup: Larry wondering if Sandy even has good seats
jokes.punchline: Sandy has courtside seats but gave Larry terrible ones while sitting with David Spade
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation - not only did Sandy shaft Larry, he's enjoying the good seats himself. David Spade adds insult to injury.

jokes.text: HE SCREENED MY CALL. HOW DO YOU KNOW HE SCREENED YOUR CALL? I SAW HIM ON THE BINOCULARS. HE SCREENED THE CALL. YOU KNOW, I ALWAYS THOUGHT PEOPLE WERE DOING THAT WHEN I CALLED THEM. THIS JUST CONFIRMS IT.
jokes.setup: Larry calling Sandy to complain
jokes.punchline: Larry witnessing Sandy screen his call through binoculars, confirming his paranoia
jokes.explanation: Brilliant paranoia payoff - Larry's worst fears confirmed in real-time through binoculars. Perfect character-driven comedy.

jokes.text: IT IS TOTALLY AND COMPLETELY UNACCEPTABLE THAT YOU WOULD GRILL MY DAUGHTER GRACIE ABOUT WHETHER I TOOK HER TO A BIRTHDAY PARTY. AND THEN YOU CALL ME A LIAR?
jokes.setup: The cast confronting Larry about his behavior
jokes.punchline: Julia's righteous anger about Larry interrogating her child
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and callback - Larry's inappropriate behavior comes back to bite him. Julia's anger is justified.

jokes.text: DO YOU ALSO FIND IT INTERESTING THAT, IN FACT, I HAVE TWO DAUGHTERS? GRACE AND MARY. I TOOK MARY TO THE BIRTHDAY PARTY. THAT'S INTERESTING.
jokes.setup: Larry thinking he caught Julia in a lie
jokes.punchline: Julia revealing Larry confused her two daughters, making him the idiot
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - Larry's detective work backfires completely. Perfect structure and character comeuppance.

jokes.text: A TIP IS A SOLO, LARRY. NO, IT'S NOT! THANK YOU. THANK YOU. SOLO. YOU TIP IN CONCERT! A TIP IS A SOLO. WHAT IS THE BIG SECRET? HE'S GOT A BIG SECRET! WHAT IS THE BIG SECRET?!
jokes.setup: The tip argument continuing
jokes.punchline: Jason's dramatic declaration that 'a tip is a solo' while Larry becomes increasingly unhinged
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of the tip argument. 'A tip is a solo' is perfectly quotable and captures the absurd philosophy clash.

jokes.text: YOU CAN'T GIVE A BEGRUDGING APOLOGY. YOU'VE GOTTA GIVE A SINCERE APOLOGY. I'LL GO SOMEWHERE BETWEEN BEGRUDGING AND SINCERE. HOW'S THAT? SOMEWHERE BETWEEN? WHERE BETWEEN?
jokes.setup: Jeff trying to coach Larry on apologizing
jokes.punchline: Larry's absurd attempt to quantify partial sincerity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry logic - trying to mathematically calibrate emotional sincerity. Excellent character-driven absurdism.

jokes.text: IF I FUCK UP THE APOLOGY, THEN I'LL APOLOGIZE FOR THE BAD APOLOGY. YOU CAN'T APOLOGIZE FOR AN APOLOGY! YOU GET ONE SHOT, THAT'S IT.
jokes.setup: Larry's backup plan for his apology
jokes.punchline: Jerry's exasperation at Larry's meta-apology concept
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-comedy - apologizing for an apology. Larry's circular logic vs. Jerry's practical wisdom.

jokes.text: NO 'THANK YOU, LARRY, FOR SAVING MY LIFE'? SAVING MY LIFE? HOW'D YOU SAVE MY LIFE? YOU DIDN'T SAVE MY LIFE. I DIAGNOSED YOUR ILLNESS.
jokes.setup: Larry expecting gratitude for the Lyme disease diagnosis
jokes.punchline: Susie's incredulity at Larry's self-aggrandizing 'life-saving' claim
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Larry's need for recognition vs. Susie's practical view. Callback works well.

jokes.text: NEXT TIME I'M NOT GONNA SAY ANYTHING, OKAY?! I'M GONNA KEEP MY MOUTH SHUT AND LET YOU DIE! YOU WANT ME TO DIE? GET THE HELL OUT OF HERE. YOU KNOW WHAT? YOU'VE GOT A LITTLE SPOT ON YOUR FOREHEAD. GET THE HELL OUT OF HERE. DON'T GET IT CHECKED OUT. IT'S NOT SKIN CANCER.
jokes.setup: Larry and Susie fighting about gratitude
jokes.punchline: Larry's petty revenge - pointing out a potentially cancerous spot while telling her not to check it
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy escalation - Larry's ultimate petty revenge. Perfect character moment and excellent structure.

jokes.text: IT'S ABOUT AS SORRY AS I CAN GET. I GUESS MY QUESTION IS: WAS IT SORRY ENOUGH? THAT'S IT? NO GOOD? TOO BEGRUDGING? VERY BEGRUDGING.
jokes.setup: Larry's attempted apology to Sandy
jokes.punchline: Larry's completely insincere, self-aware non-apology
jokes.explanation: Perfect anti-apology - Larry's complete inability to be genuinely sorry. Great character consistency and cringe factor.

jokes.text: I WANT TO PUT THE KIBOSH ON OUR RELATIONSHIP. I CAN SEE WHERE THIS ROAD IS GOING AND I DON'T LIKE IT, SO WE'RE DONE. THE REUNION SHOW IS OFF.
jokes.setup: Sandy's reaction to Larry's terrible apology
jokes.punchline: Sandy's definitive shutdown using 'kibosh' - ultimate executive dismissal
jokes.explanation: Good executive character moment - Sandy's professional but final dismissal. 'Kibosh' is perfect word choice.

jokes.text: HAVE YOU BEEN IN ANY TALL GRASS RECENTLY? IN THE HAMPTONS OVER THE WEEKEND, YES, WITH MY KID. WHY? NO REASON.
jokes.setup: Sandy mentioning his health problems
jokes.punchline: Larry casually setting up potential Lyme disease diagnosis then backing away
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy callback - Larry could help Sandy but chooses petty revenge instead. Perfect economy.

jokes.text: LARRY, YOU SAVED MY LIFE. AH. NOW GO DO WHAT YOU DO. OKAY. OKAY.
jokes.setup: Larry apparently got his apology right this time
jokes.punchline: Sandy's complete 180 in attitude, now praising Larry as a life-saver
jokes.explanation: Brilliant ironic reversal - Sandy now loves Larry, presumably for diagnosing his Lyme disease. Great economy and callback.

jokes.text: LARRY, I WANT YOU BACK. LET'S GO HOME AND MAKE LOVE.
jokes.setup: The reunion show back on track
jokes.punchline: Cheryl's dramatic romantic declaration during filming
jokes.explanation: Good payoff for Larry's entire scheme - his plan actually works. Perfect economy and emotional peak.

jokes.text: AND I SAID, 'YOU ARE ABSOLUTELY OUR DREAM CAST FOR GEORGE'S EX-WIFE.' And she's gonna do it! WHAT? I'm gonna put her on. Hold on.
jokes.setup: Jerry's chance encounter with Meg Ryan
jokes.punchline: Jerry immediately offering her the role that was meant for Cheryl
jokes.explanation: Excellent setup for disaster - Jerry's enthusiasm destroys Larry's entire plan. Great escalation and character-driven chaos.
